task_context.description: This is a multi-objective engineering optimization task that involves designing and comparing electromagnetic calorimeter configurations to find the optimal balance between detector performance and cost. The task requires simulating three distinct calorimeter designs with different material combinations (premium PbWO4 crystals, mid-range lead-scintillator sampling, and budget iron-scintillator sampling), measuring their energy resolution performance across multiple beam energies, calculating total material costs based on provided cost models, and determining which configuration provides the best energy resolution per dollar spent. The task combines physics simulation, performance characterization, cost analysis, and comparative evaluation to inform detector design decisions under budget constraints.
task_context.goal: Find the electromagnetic calorimeter configuration that provides the best energy resolution per dollar by comparing performance-to-cost ratios across three different design approaches
constraints: All designs must target approximately 20 radiation lengths total depth
Must test exactly 3 configurations (premium, mid-range, budget)
Energy sweep limited to 1, 5, and 20 GeV for tractable simulation
Must use electrons (e-) in Geant4 particle gun
Cost constraints based on provided material cost model
